title: ClipForm score fields allow 1–10 but data model expects 1–3 per dimension
steps_to_reproduce: Observe score fields: Difficulty Score, Signal Clarity, Noise Level, Context Dependency, Replication Difficulty
Note that each field accepts values 1–10
expected: Per shared-knowledge.md difficulty scoring system: each of the 4 dimension fields (Signal Clarity, Noise Level, Context Dependency, Replication Difficulty) should accept 1–3 only. Sum 4–5 = Beginner, 6–8 = Intermediate, 9–12 = Advanced.
actual: ClipForm uses min=1 max=10 for all score fields, and defaults all to 5. An admin can enter values outside the 1–3 range that violate the intended scoring model.
impact: Clips seeded via this form will have inconsistent difficulty scores that do not match the established taxonomy. The library filter and difficulty display may be misleading.
fix_suggestion: Change dimension score fields (signalClarity, noiseLevel, contextDependency, replicationDifficulty) to min=1 max=3; change difficultyScore to min=4 max=12; update defaults accordingly.
